title: Combine Tens and Ones Separately
description: Adds two two-digit numbers by combining tens with tens and ones with ones.
response: Add tens to get a tens total, add ones to get a ones total, then combine (composing a ten if needed).
label: Principle
notes: May involve composing an additional ten.

title: Decompose Addends to Form Equivalent Expression
description: Breaks apart addends into tens and ones to create an equivalent expression with the same total.
condition: Given a sum (e.g., 26 + 17) and alternative decomposed expressions.
response: Recognize or construct expressions that decompose numbers into tens and ones but preserve total value.
label: Principle

title: Identify Equivalent Sums
description: Determines whether two addition expressions have the same total value.
condition: Two or more addition expressions are presented for comparison.
response: Evaluate or reason about each expression and select those with equal sums.
label: Concept

title: Interpret Tens Digit as Tens Value
description: Understands that a digit in the tens place represents that many tens (groups of 10).
condition: A two-digit number with a nonzero tens digit (e.g., 46).
response: Interpret the tens digit as that many groups of 10 (e.g., 4 means 40).
label: Concept

title: Recognize Place Value Addition Error
description: Identifies the error of adding tens digits as if they were ones without assigning place value.
condition: Work shows tens digits added without multiplying by 10 (e.g., 1 + 4 = 5 instead of 10 + 40 = 50).
response: State that the tens must represent groups of 10 and correct the value.
label: Principle
